chip_reasoning: HOLD the TC chip / let it expire. Gemini's TC anchor (Haaland) is CONFIRMED OUT and would score zero. Sim-lab models the TC gain on the best available target (Bowen) at only ~6.2 pts over a standard captain, below the usual TC threshold, and GW38 is a rotation minefield with no elite double-fixture banker. Bowen as a standard (x2) captain is the correct, lower-variance play.
key_decisions: Raya (ARS GK) benched OUT of the GW38 matchday squad — forced to start Sels (NFO), the only available, confirmed-starting GK in the squad and the only non-zero xPts GK.
Captain Bowen (WHU): sim-lab EV 12.41 captained, 53.6% haul, must-win relegation decider vs Leeds at home, max motivation. Overrides Gemini's invalid Haaland (C).

Vice Salah (M.Salah, LIV): I prefer Salah over sim-lab's Mbeumo as VC — confirmed farewell-match starter, home vs nothing-to-play-for Palace, comparable EV (10.10) with a higher floor and lower rotation risk.

Back three is forced: Gabriel (season-ending surgery, OUT) and Aina (ruled out of the finale) are flagged, Gvardiol is benched at City. With only Munoz truly nailed, I field Munoz + Gvardiol + Aina as the least-bad legal back three in a 3-4-3 and pack the attack.
Started Rogers despite ~55% bench risk: sub-8% EO differential in an open AVL v City game — any return is net rank gain from 7th, and there is no better available midfield body to replace him.
Benched the dead/unavailable assets (Raya, Gabriel, Haaland) plus Kluivert (medium rotation risk, no fixture edge).
summary: Claude's independent GW38 call: captain Bowen (sim EV 12.41, 53.6% haul) in a must-win West Ham relegation decider, with Salah as the higher-floor vice in his Liverpool farewell at home to Palace. No transfers — final GW, bank irrelevant. Critically, Raya is benched so Sels is the forced GK start, and the back three is a forced least-bad selection (Munoz, Gvardiol, Aina) because Gabriel and Aina are flagged out and Gvardiol benched. TC chip is held/expired: its only legal anchor (Bowen) clears the threshold by only ~6.2 pts and Gemini's Haaland TC would score zero. Lean into owned sub-8% differentials (Rogers, Mateta) to climb from 7th in a finale where the Haaland/Gabriel template is hollow.
